KPI Bluebook

Key Performance Indicators:

  • kpi flare recovery pct
  • SOx Emissions Concentration (kg/m3
  • Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio
  • Cooling Water Delta-T (°C
  • Yield Percentage (%
  • Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF
  • On-Spec Rate (%
  • Polymer Melt Flow Index (MFI) Variance
  • Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
  • Daily Feedstock Throughput (tons/day
  • OEE %
  • Specific Catalyst Cost ($/ton
KPI Bluebook
  • Cooling Water Delta-T (°C
  • View page source

Cooling Water Delta-T (°C

\[\text{}\]
Open in: VS Code PyCharm Edit
Description
  • Cooling Water Delta-T measures the temperature difference between the inlet and outlet of cooling water systems
  • indicating the effectiveness of heat exchange processes
Objective
  • The objective of monitoring Cooling Water Delta-T is to ensure optimal cooling system performance
  • which is crucial for maintaining equipment efficiency and preventing overheating
Formula

The Cooling Water Delta-T is calculated by subtracting the inlet temperature of the cooling water from its outlet temperature.

$$ \mathit{Result} = \mathit{Outlet\,C} - \mathit{Inlet\,C;\,\}\,\}} $$
Used in KPI(s)
  • This KPI is used in various performance indicators related to cooling system efficiency and thermal management in oil
  • gas
  • and petrochemical operations
Input Measure
  • The input measures for this KPI are the inlet and outlet temperatures of the cooling water
Unit of Measure The unit of measure for Cooling Water Delta-T is degrees Celsius.
Reporting Source Data for this KPI is typically sourced from temperature sensors installed in the cooling water system.
Comments Monitoring Cooling Water Delta-T is essential for identifying potential issues in cooling systems, such as fouling or inadequate flow rates, which can impact overall operational efficiency.

Code Context

1// KPI: Cooling Water Delta-T (°C)

public static class CoolingWater {

public static double DeltaT(double inlet_c, double outlet_c) {

// ΔT = outlet - inlet return outlet_c - inlet_c;

}

}
